page: Microsoft Azure D12_v2 is a Dv2-series virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features 4 physical cores with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per vCPU, alongside 205 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data indicates that the D12_v2 exhibits limited processing capabilities. In stress-ng and Geekbench single-core testing, the instance scores in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read performance for both small and large blocks, though large block write bandwidth achieves an average rating. Cryptographic and compression workloads, such as gzip and bzip3 decompression, consistently rank in the lower percentiles.

The primary tradeoff of the D12_v2 is its older hardware generation, which results in lower computational efficiency compared to modern architectures. However, the generous memory allocation of 28.0 GB relative to its 4 vCPUs offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high CPU throughput. This profile is suitable for legacy enterprise applications, lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and small-scale memory-intensive tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D12_v2 is a Dv2-series virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance provides 28.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate that the server resides in the lower tiers for computational speed, with single-core and multi-core processing scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory read bandwidth is similarly constrained, though large block write bandwidth remains average. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio paired with modest CPU performance, the D12_v2 is best suited for memory-bound workloads with low CPU demands, such as small databases, basic web serving, and legacy enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D12_v2 is a Dv2-series virtual machine with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, offering a high memory-to-core ratio for legacy and memory-bound workloads.

tagline: An x86 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and integrated local storage for memory-bound legacy workloads.
